context: Tests whether the agent correctly handles the RGB (0,0,0) no-op firmware quirk by using near-black (1,1,1) for segment clearing, ensures all 12 physical segments are addressed at shutdown, and avoids phantom segments.

checklist.name: Near-black for shutdown
checklist.description: The shutdown/clear function uses rgb=(1,1,1) or equivalent near-black (any non-zero RGB tuple where all components are > 0, matching the plugin's recommendation of (1,1,1)) instead of rgb=(0,0,0) when clearing segments at session end

checklist.name: All 12 segments cleared
checklist.description: The shutdown call targets all physical segments 0..11 (e.g. range(12) or [0,1,2,...,11]) — does NOT stop at fewer than 12 or include indices 12+

checklist.name: Zero-value quirk explained
checklist.description: A comment, docstring, or notes section explains that rgb=(0,0,0) may be treated as a no-op by some firmware and that non-zero near-black is used to reliably darken segments

checklist.name: No phantom segments
checklist.description: No API call in the script includes segment indices 12, 13, or 14

checklist.name: Shutdown on interrupt/exception
checklist.description: The script uses try/finally (or atexit or signal handler) so that the near-black clear-all call executes even when KeyboardInterrupt or an exception terminates the loop
